summary: An arc or sector is just a fraction of the whole circle — the central angle over 360° tells you exactly which fraction.
explanation: A central angle of \(\theta\) degrees marks off a fraction \(\theta/360\) of the entire circle. Arc length is that same fraction of the full circumference, and sector area is that same fraction of the full area. The inscribed angle theorem is a distinct but related idea: an inscribed angle (vertex on the circle) is always exactly half of the central angle that subtends the same arc — in particular, an inscribed angle subtending a diameter is always exactly 90°. When a problem gives you an inscribed angle, double it to get the central angle (or the arc measure) before applying the arc/sector fraction formulas.
